items.title: Hospital call from Melbourne’s outer north leaders

children.content: A group of councils in Melbourne’s north is calling on the state government to spend $45 million to buy land to begin planning for a hospital and train station.

gallery.altText: Man in blue blazer and white shirt standing next to man in navy jumper

thumbnail.altText: Man in blue blazer and white shirt standing next to man in navy jumper

items.title: Former police officer steps aside from country football over sexual assault charges

children.content: A female footballer has "stepped aside" from her country Victoria club after sexual assault allegations against her were revealed.

image.altText: A female footballer raises both arms on the field.

items.title: Police investigate spectator brawl during country Victorian football match

children.content: Victoria Police is investigating an off-field fight at a local football match in Ararat over the weekend, sparking concerns about "dreadful" behaviour at grassroots sporting events.

image.altText: A security camera still of a man punching someone in the crowd at a football game

items.title: Disgraced former AFL footballer jailed for pool fraud

children.content: An ex-footballer has been locked up after a jury found him guilty of defrauding multiple families with his regional pool business.

gallery.altText: A sketch of a bald Caucasian man in prison green

thumbnail.altText: A sketch of a bald Caucasian man in prison green

items.title: Bold BBL plan within reach after crunch meeting between CA and states

children.content: A meeting between Cricket Australia and the six state bodies has ended in significant steps being taken towards BBL privatisation.

image.altText: a batter dressed in green hits the ball, while a keeper in red is behind him at the stumps

items.title: Where are the Kelpies? Frustration grows as progress dips in men's netball

children.content: Despite men's netballers making a valuable contribution to the Aussie Diamonds pathway and Super Netball competition, progress between the two genders at the international level has largely been scaled back and unexplained.

image.altText: Two players wear green and gold uniforms and stand with their hands on this hips

items.title: Victorian council rejects calls to install CCTV at crime 'hotspots'

children.content: Parents are working to petition a west Victorian council to install CCTV cameras at a skate park that police are calling a crime "hotspot". The council says taking on a public safety role is "unreasonable".

image.altText: Wide shot of pink skate park with a bowl, light blue sky, black fence in the foreground.

items.title: Suburb with worst library access in Melbourne has just two for 230,000 locals

children.content: Local councils and Public Libraries Victoria say the way public libraries are funded across Victoria needs reform, with communities in Melbourne's growth corridors described as "chronically underfunded".

image.altText: Two boys stand facing a Melton Library access point self-serve borrowing spot.

items.title: Icy atmosphere as Victorian MPs raise funds for MND

children.content: State government and opposition MPs take the plunge to raise tens of thousands of dollars for motor neurone disease research just days after Neale Daniher's state funeral.

gallery.altText: A man in a bright pink, blue, yellow and black top holds his mouth open wide with his fists in the air inside a cage.
A drenched woman stands chest height in water inside a steel cage as people watch on.
A man in a blue beanie talks into a microphone beside a man in a wig and khaki top and holding a blow up crocodile.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a bright pink, blue, yellow and black top holds his mouth open wide with his fists in the air inside a cage.
A drenched woman stands chest height in water inside a steel cage as people watch on.
A man in a blue beanie talks into a microphone beside a man in a wig and khaki top and holding a blow up crocodile.

items.title: Jubilant fans pack Federation Square to celebrate Socceroos win

children.content: In Melbourne, Federation Square became the epicentre of fan festivities, with capacity crowds packing into the CBD venue for a Socceroos viewing party.

articles.title: The Socceroos' greatest ever World Cup gamble just paid off

children.content: With his first World Cup team selection, Tony Popovic produced one of the biggest shocks in Socceroos' history. After 90 minutes against Türkiye, his gamble was vindicated.

image.altText: Patrick Beach punches the air.

gallery.altText: Soccer fans, including Ethan, have poured into Melbourne's Federation Square.
A huge crowd seen at Federation Square
A Socceroo fan shows her allegiance with face paint

thumbnail.altText: Soccer fans, including Ethan, have poured into Melbourne's Federation Square.
A huge crowd seen at Federation Square
A Socceroo fan shows her allegiance with face paint

items.title: Hundreds of regional firefighters rally against levy in Bendigo

children.content: Hundreds of regional Victorian firefighters turn out for a rally in Bendigo, calling on the state government to scrap a controversial levy after saying they have been "left behind".

image.altText: A group of people wearing yellow CFA jackets.

items.title: Driver dies after crashing into pole at Calder Park

children.content: A driver has died at Calder Park, in Melbourne's north-west, after crashing into a pole.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Experts say places of worship overlooked in planning for new suburbs

children.content: A plan to relocate a church into a green wedge has sparked controversy in the community, as places of worship report being excluded from development plans.

image.altText: two men looking at each other in a church, with a cross in the background

items.title: Early childhood educators burnt out, stretched and 'paid peanuts'

children.content: Early childhood operators say increased load on educators is taking a "huge toll" on their mental health and safety.

image.altText: Kids toys on the floor.

items.title: Victorian opposition leader says Pauline Hanson has not approached her

children.content: The One Nation leader last night told a political fundraiser she would work with the Coalition to oust Labor at the upcoming state election.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson pictured at a One Nation political fundraiser

items.title: Ryan kicks six as Saints get home in GWS thriller at Docklands

children.content: Liam Ryan kicks half a dozen goals as St Kilda earns a thrilling eight-point win over Greater Western Sydney, after Brisbane's victory in Hobart over the Tigers.

image.altText: An AFL player in red and white and black celebrates a goal with arms outstretched

items.title: What Australia would have looked like when the dinosaurs roamed

children.content: Palaeontologists say evidence pointing to Australia's polar past shows that many dinosaurs roamed the country when the south east was still within the Antarctic Circle.

gallery.altText: Artist's impression of a large carnivorous dinosaur hunting two smaller dinosaurs.

thumbnail.altText: Artist's impression of a large carnivorous dinosaur hunting two smaller dinosaurs.

items.title: Grandfather of child rescued from Syria pleads for 'stability, love, care'

children.content: Jasmine was five years old and so traumatised that when she woke up trying to scream, she couldn't make a sound.

image.altText: Illustration shows a child holding a doll and the hand of an adult in a refugee camp.

items.title: 'Lambo Guy' Adrian Portelli sues former minister over lottery comments

children.content: Melbourne "rewards club" lottery billionaire Adrian Portelli has launched a defamation lawsuit against former SA minister Andrea Michaels over comments she made about a $3.5 million home prize draw.

image.altText: A man holding 100 dollar bills in his hand

items.title: No Medicare, no family, no visa — these are the babies Australia doesn't count

children.content: For the Pacific Islander women who harvest the fruit and vegetables that stock Australia’s supermarket shelves, motherhood here can mean injury, debt, secrecy and fear.

gallery.altText: illustrations of a woman with pregnant belly picking grapes

thumbnail.altText: illustrations of a woman with pregnant belly picking grapes

items.title: Farmers worry 70km dingo fence 'not enough' to protect sheep

children.content: Farmers in Victoria’s north-west say a new dingo exclusion fence will only push the problem of livestock predation onto their neighbours.

image.altText: Dingoes prowl along an exclusion fence

items.title: Swans steal thriller with spirited second-half comeback against Port

children.content: Sydney's big guns all stand up tall in the final quarter to help secure a three-point win over Port Adelaide in Adelaide as North Melbourne escapes against West Coast.

image.altText: Isaac Heeney handballs while being tackled

items.title: Australian sprinter's death in Thailand came weeks after her 25th birthday

children.content: The brother of Stawell Gift finalist Jemma Stapleton remembers her "bright smile" and expresses gratitude for the outpouring of grief and support.

image.altText: An athlete holds her hands up next to head and smiles.

items.title: Murderer's mum uses ChatGPT to tell court of son's 'caring nature'

children.content: The mother of a man found guilty of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston has told the Supreme Court her son has always been polite and considerate.

image.altText: A close-up selfie of a man in a car.

items.title: Hundreds of 'extraordinary' Cartier jewellery pieces on display at NGV

children.content: An exhibition at the National Gallery of Victoria in Melbourne features glittering tiaras and dazzling necklaces worn by Hollywood icons such as Elizabeth Taylor and Grace Kelly.

gallery.altText: Elizabeth Taylor wearing a Cartier necklace and earrings at the Tropicana Hotel in Las Vegas in 1958
Inside the NGV's colourful Cartier exhibition

thumbnail.altText: Elizabeth Taylor wearing a Cartier necklace and earrings at the Tropicana Hotel in Las Vegas in 1958
Inside the NGV's colourful Cartier exhibition

items.title: Firefighters union behind push to stop release of anti-corruption report

children.content: The United Firefighters Union and its secretary Peter Marshall are behind a legal attempt to stop a report by Victoria's anti-corruption watchdog from being published.

image.altText: Fire union secretary Peter Marshall pictured in his uniform speaking at a rally.

items.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion dollar claim figure

children.content: Hundreds of businesses and organisations are part of a major Victorian Supreme Court class action against chemical manufacturer 3M.

image.altText: An industrial plant in the Melbourne suburb of Altona.

items.title: VCE students in limbo as Chinese language school enters administration

children.content: Dozens of students completing VCE Chinese language subjects face an uncertain future after a Chinese community language school goes into administration.

image.altText: A female student completes her exam using a laptop.

items.title: Melbourne man charged with manufacturing firearms, drug trafficking

children.content: The 51-year-old man has been charged after police allegedly found multiple firearms, imitation firearms, drugs and a machete during a search at a property in Melbourne's west.

image.altText: Two police officers escort a man in handcuffs down a driveway.

items.title: Critics say Victoria is struggling to house growing prison population

children.content: The state's bail laws were changed after a high-profile death in custody. Advocates are warning that last year's tightening of the Bail Act could see history repeat itself.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a young woman with long dark hair.

items.title: Recruiting begins for Victoria Police's first regional academy

children.content: Victoria Police's new regional training academy in Mildura in the state's north-west will be a full 25-week training program available for up to 25 recruits.

image.altText: Six police officers stand near a police car and a colourful mural which says the word Mildura

items.title: Daylesford pub had no outdoor dining permit at time of deadly crash, court told

children.content: A Victorian court hears that a regional pub where five people died when a driver had a hypoglycaemic episode, crashing his car through diners, did not have a valid outdoor dining permit at the time of the crash.

image.altText: Two water jugs, paper cups and a bucket sit on a table that has a broken wooden stool.

items.title: Former police officer jailed for harassing woman online

children.content: A former Victoria Police officer is sentenced to six months in prison after repeatedly harassing a woman on social media.

image.altText: Former Victoria Police officer Craig Lineham stands outside in an undated social media photo.

items.title: Police up ante after prisoner's escape from brother's Melbourne funeral

children.content: A prisoner who absconded from his brother's funeral in Melbourne's north-west on Anzac Day continues to evade detection.

image.altText: CCTV footage of Orijol Rukaj inside a corrections facility, whch has carpet and small chairs and tables

items.title: More couples opt for micro-weddings and elopements amid cost-of-living crisis

children.content: Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding for just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000.

gallery.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

thumbnail.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.
